pattern: Matching with '?' and '*' is a two-dimensional DP where '*' can absorb any run of characters, captured by carrying a single 'star covers everything so far' relation.
hints: A '?' matches exactly one character while '*' can match an empty run or any number of characters.
Let dp[i][j] mean the first i characters of s match the first j of p; a '*' lets dp[i][j] inherit from either dp[i-1][j] or dp[i][j-1].

Initialize dp[0][j] true while p[:j] is all stars; for a literal or '?' require the previous diagonal and a character match, and for '*' take the OR of dropping the star or consuming one more character of s.

edge_inputs.description: single star matches the whole string
question mark matches one but the literal then fails
star must be allowed to match an empty run
empty pattern cannot match a nonempty string
stars and literals interleaved where greedy fails

stress.description: short strings and patterns over a tiny alphabet with frequent wildcards
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    cap = min(30, max(2, size))
    sn = rng.randrange(0, cap + 1)
    s = ''.join(rng.choice('ab') for _ in range(sn))
    pn = rng.randrange(0, cap + 1)
    p = ''.join(rng.choice(['a', 'b', '?', '*']) for _ in range(pn))
    return [s, p]
stress.note: tiny alphabet with many stars stresses the empty-run and absorb branches
